# BlueDeck

> BlueDeck is a multi-terminal, agent-native development tool. It runs up to 16 real terminals (PTYs) per workspace in a grid inside a single window, and lets you launch and command coding agents — Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI and Aider — in each pane. It is built on Rust + Tauri 2, so it uses the OS WebView for roughly 1/10 the memory of an Electron app and ships as a few-MB native binary. Tagline: "One deck. Every agent."

## Key facts

- Category: developer tool / terminal / AI coding-agent orchestrator
- Platforms: macOS (Apple Silicon) and Windows (x64); both auto-update
- Latest version: v0.1.12
- Tech: Rust + Tauri 2 core, real PTYs, xterm.js (WebGL) terminal renderer (same as VS Code)
- Supported agents (auto-detected): Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Aider
- Website: https://cslim0527.github.io/bluedeck-site/
- Download: https://cslim0527.github.io/bluedeck-site/download.html
- Documentation: https://cslim0527.github.io/bluedeck-site/docs.html
- Languages: English (default) and Korean

## What makes it different

- Up to 16 real terminals per workspace in an automatic grid — real PTYs, not emulated.
- Run and command multiple coding agents side by side from one window.
- Agent Hub: an embedded MCP server (http://127.0.0.1:41720/mcp) auto-registered with Claude Code, letting agents read each other's output and pass prompts (list_agents, send_to_agent, read_agent_output) for autonomous agent-to-agent collaboration.
- Broadcast mode: send one prompt to every terminal in a workspace at once.
- Git worktree isolation: each pane can get its own worktree + branch so parallel agents never touch each other's files.
- Diff review: inspect a colored unified diff per pane and squash-merge back with one click. After the merge, one confirmation removes the worktree and deletes its branch.
- Agent status awareness: each pane is tracked as working / needs-input / idle, with macOS notifications when the app is in the background. A topbar pill counts waiting agents; Cmd+J jumps to the next one. Notification scope is configurable in Settings, and clicking a needs-input notification jumps to that terminal.
- Keyboard-first control: Cmd+1-9 focuses a terminal, Cmd+J jumps to the terminal waiting for input, Cmd+K opens a command palette covering every action, and Cmd+/ shows an in-app shortcut cheat sheet.
- In-app bug reporting: file a GitHub issue from inside the app (one-time device-code auth, optional diagnostics, browser fallback).
- Session restore: layout and running agents are restored on reopen; Claude Code panes resume via `claude --continue`.

## Pages

- [Home](https://cslim0527.github.io/bluedeck-site/): overview, features, how it works, pricing.
- [Download](https://cslim0527.github.io/bluedeck-site/download.html): choose macOS (.dmg) or Windows (.exe / .msi). Installers are hosted at the public GitHub repo `cslim0527/blue-deck-releases` (releases/latest).
- [Documentation](https://cslim0527.github.io/bluedeck-site/docs.html): install, workspaces, terminal grid, agents, agent dispatch & auto-approve, prompt bar & broadcast, command palette, status awareness, git worktree isolation, diff review & post-merge cleanup, Agent Hub (MCP), session restore, bug reporting, keyboard shortcuts, licensing, updates, FAQ.

## Pricing

- Lite (free): the core terminal after the 14-day trial — 1 workspace × 2 terminals, agent launch, IME, prompt bar, layout restore.
- Pro (per-device license): the full multi-agent deck — status awareness, git worktree isolation, diff review, Agent Hub, broadcast, auto-approve and conversation resume.
- Every download includes a 14-day full trial (no credit card). Paid features are enforced in the Rust core.

## FAQ

- Which agents are supported? Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI and Aider are auto-detected; any other CLI can be run by hand in a pane.
- Is my code sent anywhere? No. Terminals run locally, and the Agent Hub MCP server binds to 127.0.0.1 (localhost) only.
- Does it work offline? Yes, once a license is activated it is stored locally; it revalidates online in the background when it can.
- Which platforms are supported? macOS on Apple Silicon and Windows on x64. Both auto-update.
